Version.Name: P-Glycoprotein activity model (NIC)
Version.Summary: Classification model for P-Glycoprotein inhibition / substrate activity

Guide.Description: The model provides a qualitative prediction of P-Glycoprotein inhibition/substrate activity. It is implemented inside the VEGA online platform, accessible at:http://www.vega-qsar.eu/. The model has been implemented by the National Institute of Chemistry (Slovenia)
Guide.Model: The model has been built as a Counter Propagation Artificial Neural Network (CP ANN). The neural network output consists of three values labeled as “Inhibitor”, “Substrate” and “Non active”, all in the range [0,1] and with sum equal to 1; they represent the probability that the neuron in which the predicted compound falls belongs to the class of inhibitor, substrate or non active compounds. The higher between these values leads to the prediction, if the value itself is higher than 0.5, otherwise the target compound is not predicted. Full reference and details of the used formulas can be found in: Mora Lagares, L.; Minovski, N.; Novič, M. Multiclass Classifier for P-Glycoprotein Substrates, Inhibitors, and Non-Active Compounds. Molecules 2019, 24, 2006. Mora Lagares, L.; Minovski, N.; Caballero Alfonso, A.Y.; Benfenati, E.; Wellens, S.; Culot, M.; Gosselet, F.; Novič, M. Homology Modeling of the Human P-glycoprotein (ABCB1) and Insights into Ligand Binding through Molecular Docking Studies. Int. J. Mol. Sci. 2020, 21, 4058. The descriptors used by the CPANN are the following: - H%: percentage of H atoms - nR07: number of 7-membered rings - D/Dtr11: distance/detour ring index of order 11 - MWC01: molecular walk count of order 1 - X2A: average connectivity index of order 2 - SIC3: Structural Information Content index (neighborhood symmetry of 3-order) - VE1sign_B(s): coefficient sum of the last eigenvector from Burden matrix weighted by I-State - ATSC7m: Centred Broto-Moreau autocorrelation of lag 7 weighted by mass - MATS6v: Moran autocorrelation of lag 6 weighted by van der Waals volume - GATS4s: Geary autocorrelation of lag 4 weighted by I-state - P_VSA_LogP_3: P_VSA-like on LogP, bin 3 - P_VSA_ppp_D: P_VSA-like on potential pharmacophore points, D - hydrogen-bond donor - nRCOOR: number of esters (aliphatic) - nArCONHR: number of secondary amides (aromatic) - nArCO: number of ketones (aromatic) - H-048: H attached to C2(sp3)/C1(sp2)/C0(sp) - SdsCH: Sum of dsCH E-states - CATS2D_01_DN: CATS2D Donor-Negative at lag 01 2 - CATS2D_05_PP: CATS2D Positive-Positive at lag 05 - CATS2D_02_PL: CATS2D Positive-Lipophilic at lag 02 - B07[O-F]: Presence/absence of O - F at topological distance 7 - F01[C-C]: Frequency of C - C at topological distance 1 - F02[C-O]: Frequency of C - O at topological distance 2 - F04[C-P]: Frequency of C - P at topological distance 4 - F04[C-Br]: Frequency of C - Br at topological distance 4 - F07[O-F]: Frequency of O - F at topological distance 7 The descriptors were calculated, in the original model, by means of Dragon software and are now entirely calculated by an in-house software module in which they are implemented as described in: R. Todeschini and V. Consonni, Molecular Descriptors for Chemoinformatics, Wiley-VCH, 2009. The model provides, as an additional information, the value of the Euclidean distance of the molecular descriptors of the input molecule from the weights of the neuron in which the molecule is classified. The weights represent the center of the neuron (in the space of molecular descriptors), so the calculated distance can be used as a further evaluation regarding the applicability domain, besides the standard AD provided for all VEGA models (see section 1.3). The largest distance value found in the original data is 5.45 and this value can be used as a threshold: higher values suggest that the molecule could be out of the applicability domain of the model.

AD.Name: Similar_Molecules
AD.DescriptionRangeTop: strongly similar compounds with known experimental value in the training set have been found
AD.DescriptionRangeMid: only moderately similar compounds with known experimental value in the training set have been found
AD.DescriptionRangeBottom: no similar compounds with known experimental value in the training set have been found

AD.Name: Accuracy
AD.DescriptionRangeTop: accuracy of prediction for similar molecules found in the training set is good
AD.DescriptionRangeMid: accuracy of prediction for similar molecules found in the training set is not optimal
AD.DescriptionRangeBottom: accuracy of prediction for similar molecules found in the training set is not adequate

AD.Name: Concordance
AD.DescriptionRangeTop: similar molecules found in the training set have experimental values that agree with the predicted value
AD.DescriptionRangeMid: some similar molecules found in the training set have experimental values that disagree with the predicted value
AD.DescriptionRangeBottom: similar molecules found in the training set have experimental values that disagree with the predicted value

AD.Name: ACF_SimCheck
AD.DescriptionRangeTop: all atom centered fragment of the compound have been found in the compounds of the training set
AD.DescriptionRangeMid: some atom centered fragment of the compound have not been found in the compounds of the training set or are rare fragments
AD.DescriptionRangeBottom: a prominent number of atom centered fragments of the compound have not been found in the compounds of the training set or are rare fragments

AD.Name: AD_Index
AD.DescriptionRangeTop: predicted substance is into the Applicability Domain of the model
AD.DescriptionRangeMid: predicted substance could be out of the Applicability Domain of the model
AD.DescriptionRangeBottom: predicted substance is out of the the Applicability Domain of the model

AD.Name: Model_Descriptors_Range_Check
Model_Descriptors_Range_Check
AD.DescriptionRangeTop: descriptors for this compound have values inside the descriptor range of the compounds of the training set
AD.DescriptionRangeBottom: descriptors for this compound have values outside the descriptor range of the compounds of the training set

Guide.Structural_Alerts:
The following SAs have been extracted from the original dataset and are related to compounds which are nP in the water compartment: - nP alert no. 1, defined by the SMARTS: O=CC - nP alert no. 2, defined by the SMARTS: Nc1ccc(cc1) - nP alert no. 3, defined by the SMARTS: c1ccc(OC)cc1 - nP alert no. 4, defined by the SMARTS: Cc1ccccc1C - nP alert no. 5, defined by the SMARTS: c1(C)c2c(cccc2)ccc1 - nP alert no. 6, defined by the SMARTS: c1(O)c(C)cccc1 - nP alert no. 7, defined by the SMARTS: P(OC)OC - nP alert no. 8, defined by the SMARTS: C(O)C - nP alert no. 9, defined by the SMARTS: c1ccccc1O - nP alert no. 10, defined by the SMARTS: O=C - nP alert no. 11, defined by the SMARTS: C(=C)C - nP alert no. 12, defined by the SMARTS: NC - nP alert no. 13, single carboxyl acids (aliphatic), defined by the SMARTS: C(=O)([O;D1;!-])[A] - nP alert no. 14, single esters (aliphatic), defined by the SMARTS: O=[$([C;D2]),$([C;D3]C)][O;D2][C,c] - nP alert no. 15, multiple esters (aromatic), defined by the SMARTS: O=C([a])[O;D2][C,c] - nP alert no. 16, single (thio)-carbamates (aromatic), defined by the SMARTS: [$(C(=[O,S])([O,S][a])N),$(C(=[O,S])([O,S])N[a])] - nP alert no. 17, single aldehydes (aliphatic), defined by the SMARTS: [$([C;D2](=O)C)] - nP alert no. 18, single ketones (aliphatic), defined by the SMARTS: [C;D3](=O)([C])[C] - nP alert no. 19, single primary amines (aliphatic), defined by the SMARTS: [N;D1][$([C;A]);!$(C=[O,S])] - nP alert no. 20, primary amines (aromatic), defined by the SMARTS: [N;D1][a] - nP alert no. 21, single N-nitroso groups (aliphatic), defined by the SMARTS: O=NN([C,c])[C,c] - nP alert no. 22, hydroxyl groups, defined by the SMARTS: [O;D1;!-]A - nP alert no. 23, single aromatic hydroxyl, defined by the SMARTS: [O;D1;!-]a - nP alert no. 24, primary alcohol, defined by the SMARTS: [O;D1;!-][C;D2;H2][C,c]
The following SAs have been extracted from the original dataset and are related to compounds which are vP in the water compartment: - vP alert no. 1, defined by the SMARTS: c1ccc(c(c1)c2ccccc2Cl) - vP alert no. 2, defined by the SMARTS: C1(=C(C2(C3C(CC(C3))C1(C2(Cl)Cl)Cl)Cl)Cl)Cl - vP alert no. 3, defined by the SMARTS: c1cc(c(c(c1Cl)Cl)Cl) - vP alert no. 4, defined by the SMARTS: c1(c2ccccc2)ccccc1

Stats.Stats_Notes: Considering only the “Substrate” class vs the remaining (“Inhibitor” and “Non active”): ● Training set: n = 1785; Accuracy = 0.97; Specificity = 0.98; Sensitivity = 0.91 ● Test set: n = 726; Accuracy = 0.89; Specificity = 0.93; Sensitivity = 0.69
